description_long: This is a small, intricately detailed sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, characterized by an elephant head and multiple arms. The figure is seated in a cross-legged posture, with ornate carvings highlighting the trunk, headdress, and jewelry. The surface has a reddish-brown metallic appearance, with visible highlights and shadows accentuating the contours and fine details. The sculpture's back is less detailed, focusing on the outline of the headdress and arms, while the sides reveal the depth and three-dimensionality of the trunk and limbs. The overall form is compact and solid, suggesting it is cast from metal. This type of sculpture is often used as a decorative or religious object in urban or domestic settings.
description: A compact, reddish-brown metallic sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, seated with detailed carvings and multiple arms.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the back of the headdress, arms, and the overall outline of the seated figure with minimal surface detail.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the protruding trunk, arm, and the depth of the figure's profile.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the detailed face, trunk, multiple arms, and ornate carvings on the body and headdress.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the trunk's extension and the layered structure of the arms and torso.
attribute_subject_matter: Hindu deity Ganesha seated with multiple arms
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the object appears isolated against a plain background.
